invest.description: Strategic investment advisory designed for individuals seeking capital growth and passive income through UAE real estate.
invest.yieldsDesc: Dubai offers 6-8% average rental yields—among the highest globally for prime real estate markets.
invest.appreciationDesc: Strategic locations show consistent 8-15% annual appreciation, driven by infrastructure and demand growth.
invest.visaDesc: Property investments of AED 2M+ qualify for the UAE Golden Visa—10-year residency for you and your family.
invest.calcDesc: Estimate your potential returns on a Dubai property investment.
invest.calcNote: Net ROI assumes ~15% deduction for maintenance, service charges, and vacancy.
invest.whyTaxDesc: Zero income tax, zero capital gains tax, and zero property tax on residential assets.
invest.whyYieldsDesc: Prime areas consistently deliver 6-8% gross yields, outperforming London and New York.
invest.whyStabilityDesc: World-class regulatory framework, strong rule of law, and transparent real estate processes.
invest.whyLifestyleDesc: Global connectivity, safety, and an unmatched quality of life attract talent and tenants alike.
invest.goldenVisaDesc: Invest AED 2 million or more in UAE real estate and secure a 10-year renewable residency visa for yourself and your family. Enjoy the benefits of living, working, and investing in one of the world's most dynamic economies.

about.heroDesc: We are not just brokers. We are your strategic partners in asset acquisition and portfolio management.
about.privateOfficeDesc: Asas operates like a family office for real estate, offering bespoke advisory services for High Net Worth Individuals. From portfolio structuring and off-market access to tax-efficient acquisition strategies, our Private Office delivers institutional-grade solutions with a personal touch.

buyerGuide.subtitle: Your Roadmap
buyerGuide.title: Buyer's Guide to UAE Real Estate
buyerGuide.intro: Purchasing property in the UAE is straightforward, but understanding the process ensures a smooth experience. Here's everything you need to know.
buyerGuide.step1Desc: Are you buying for personal use, rental income, or capital appreciation? Understanding your objective helps narrow the right property type, location, and budget.
buyerGuide.step2Desc: Freehold areas allow full ownership for foreign nationals. Leasehold areas offer long-term rights (typically 99 years). Your advisor will guide you to the right structure.
buyerGuide.step3Desc: Once you've selected a property, the process involves signing a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU), securing financing if needed, and completing the transfer at the Dubai Land Department.

careers.title: Build Your Career at Asas Invest
careers.description: We're always looking for talented individuals who share our passion for real estate excellence and client-first thinking. If you're driven, ambitious, and ready to make an impact, we'd love to hear from you.

insightDetail.descFallback: {{title}} — Dubai real estate insights from Asas Invest

faqBuy.q: What types of property can I buy in Dubai?
faqBuy.a: Dubai offers a wide range of property types including studios, apartments (1-4 bedrooms), penthouses, townhouses, villas, duplexes, and commercial spaces. Properties are available as off-plan (under construction with payment plans) or ready (completed and available for immediate move-in).

faqBuy.q: Which areas in Dubai allow freehold ownership for foreigners?
faqBuy.a: Foreigners can buy freehold property in over 50 designated areas including Dubai Marina, Downtown Dubai, Palm Jumeirah, JBR, Dubai Hills Estate, Business Bay, DIFC, Dubai Creek Harbour, Arabian Ranches, and Dubai Sports City among many others.

faqBuy.q: How long does it take to buy property in Dubai?
faqBuy.a: A property purchase in Dubai typically takes 2-4 weeks for ready properties and can be completed in a few days for off-plan purchases. The process involves property selection, MOU signing, NOC from developer, and DLD transfer registration.

faqInvest.q: What is the minimum investment for Dubai real estate?
faqInvest.a: You can invest in Dubai property from AED 500,000 (approximately USD 136,000). Off-plan projects often offer entry points with 10-20% down payment and flexible payment plans spread over construction and post-handover periods.

faqInvest.q: Can foreigners buy property in Dubai?
faqInvest.a: Yes. Foreigners can purchase freehold property in designated areas across Dubai including Dubai Marina, Downtown Dubai, Palm Jumeirah, Dubai Hills Estate, and many more communities. There are no nationality restrictions for freehold purchases.

faqInvest.q: What is the Golden Visa through property investment?
faqInvest.a: The UAE Golden Visa grants 10-year renewable residency to property investors who purchase real estate worth AED 2 million or more. The property can be off-plan or ready, and investors can include their family members.

faqInvest.q: What rental yields can I expect in Dubai?
faqInvest.a: Dubai offers average rental yields of 6-9% annually, significantly higher than most global cities. Areas like Dubai Marina, JVC, and Dubai Sports City offer some of the highest yields, while premium communities offer strong capital appreciation.

faqInvest.q: Is there property tax in Dubai?
faqInvest.a: Dubai has zero annual property tax, zero capital gains tax, and zero income tax on rental earnings. The only transaction costs are a 4% Dubai Land Department registration fee on purchase and standard service charges for building maintenance.

faqInvest.q: What is the difference between off-plan and ready properties?
faqInvest.a: Off-plan properties are purchased during construction, typically at lower prices with flexible payment plans. Ready properties are completed and available for immediate occupancy or rental income. Both offer freehold ownership for foreign investors.

faqBuyerGuide.q: How do I buy property in Dubai as a foreigner?
faqBuyerGuide.a: Foreigners can buy freehold property in designated areas across Dubai. The process involves selecting a property, signing a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU), paying a 10% deposit, obtaining a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the developer, and completing the transfer at the Dubai Land Department (DLD) with a 4% registration fee.

faqBuyerGuide.q: What are the costs of buying property in Dubai?
faqBuyerGuide.a: Total buying costs are approximately 7-8% on top of the property price. This includes 4% DLD registration fee, 2% agency commission, AED 580 DLD admin fee, AED 4,200 title deed issuance fee, and conveyancing costs. There is no stamp duty, VAT on resale properties, or annual property tax.
